problem: A square has an area of $25.$ A rectangle has the same width as the square. The length of the rectangle is double its width. What is the area of the rectangle?

classified_questions.question: Can you describe the first step in finding the area of the rectangle?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How are the dimensions of the square related to the dimensions of the rectangle?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Why does the rectangle have the same width as the square?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How did you determine the length of the rectangle to be double its width?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why the area of the rectangle is calculated by multiplying the width by the length?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other situations where the relationship between width and length could be used to find area?
classified_questions.label: e
